source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage / Crane Incident

context.region: Gulf of Mexico - Eugene Island Block 360

actions_taken: Employee evacuated by helicopter to onshore medical facility
On-site investigation conducted by BSEE Lafayette District
event.summary: On 9 December 2021 at approximately 1030 hours, a Danos rigger (IE) was injured while assisting with laying down a 1,000-pound pulley using the platform crane on Cox Operating's EI 360-E facility. The crane operator intended to lower the pulley horizontally to the deck. However, a QCP rigger unhooked the strap while the pulley was still in vertical position and pushed it to drop horizontally. The IE moved forward with his left foot in the path of the falling pulley, resulting in contact above the steel tip of his boot. The employee sustained a fractured big toe and two dislocated toes, requiring surgery with partial amputation of the big toe. This was classified as a Lost Time Accident (>3 days) with required evacuation.
key_phrases: failure to discuss load placement during pre-job safety meeting
failure to implement stop work authority
crane operator left cab during critical operation

threats.name: Premature unhoking of load before horizontal positioning
Failure to communicate load placement procedure
Crane operator absence from cab during critical lift phase
Personnel positioning in load fall path

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA)
controls.lod_basis: Administrative control to identify hazards before work begins

supporting_text: The DCO, a QCP rigger, and the IE were all involved during the lift and filled out a Job Safety Analyses (JSA) but did not discuss how the pulley would be placed on the deck.
BSEE found that the probable cause of the injury was a failure to discuss how the pulley would be lowered to the deck during the pre-job safety meeting

controls.name: Crane Operator Authority to Stop Work
controls.barrier_role: Operational control to halt unsafe conditions
controls.lod_basis: API RP 2D 3.1.5 and Cox Safety Environmental Management System policy

supporting_text: The AI found that stop work should have been implemented as a potential hazard was introduced when the QCP rigger unhooked the strap before laying the pulley horizontally with the crane.
The DCO failed to stop operations due to safety concerns as the riggers unhooked the pulley before the DCO could lower the pulley horizontally to the deck as per API RP 2D 3.1.5 Operating Practices
Whenever there is any doubt as to safety, the Crane Operator should have the authority to stop and refuse to handle loads or continue operations as safety dictates.

controls.name: Crane Operator Continuous Supervision of Load
controls.barrier_role: Operational control to maintain visual oversight during lift
controls.lod_basis: Standard crane operation practice and API RP 2D

supporting_text: The DCO had a clear view of the pulley and left the crane cab to discuss placing the pulley horizontally on the deck with the crane.
The crane operator shall review the JSA as a pre-lift discussion with all personnel involved in the upcoming lifting operation.

controls.name: Communication Protocol for Load Placement
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to ensure coordinated load handling

supporting_text: As the QCP rigger unstrapped the pulley, he pushed the top of the pulley so it would drop horizontally to the deck without communicating his intent to the IE prior to the incident.

Human Performance Error: Inattention to task: The QCP rigger unstrapped the 1,000 lbs. pulley that was standing vertically and pushed it from the top to lay it down without communicating his intent to the IE prior to the incident.

controls.name: Personnel Positioning and Load Path Awareness
controls.barrier_role: Operational control to prevent personnel in load path

supporting_text: The IE moved forward placing his left foot in the path of the pulley as it fell.
The IE moved to avoid the falling pulley, but the pulley rolled slightly toward the IE before falling. The IE failed to realize his left foot was in the path of the pulley as it fell.

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
